title: A foundation model of transcription across human cell types
data_sources: Fetal scATAC-seq atlas (Domcke et al., descartes.brotmanbaty.org)
Fetal expression atlas (Cao et al., descartes.brotmanbaty.org)
TF motif PWMs (Vierstra et al., 2,179 motifs / 282 clusters)
TF protein interaction atlas (Goos et al., mass spectrometry)

data_scope.species: human (fetal and adult primary cell types only)

core_conclusion: GET (General Expression Transformer) is an interpretable foundation model pre-trained on chromatin accessibility data across 213 human fetal and adult cell types that achieves experimental-level accuracy (Pearson r = 0.94) in predicting gene expression in previously unseen cell types, using only motif binding scores and chromatin accessibility. GET also uncovers universal and cell-type-specific transcription factor interaction networks via causal discovery and AlphaFold structural analysis, and identifies a PAX5–NR2C2 interaction in B cells that mechanistically explains a leukaemia-predisposing germline mutation.
why_it_matters: First generalizable transcription foundation model surpassing prior cell-type-specific models on leave-out cell types; cross-platform LoRA fine-tuning in minutes; causal TF interaction catalogue at 1,718 pair scale; disease-insight via PAX5 G183S leukaemia variant mechanism; fully open data and models.
why_nature: Paradigm shift from cell-type-specific supervised models to a generalizable foundation model; mechanistic insight connecting computational discovery to cancer biology; multi-modal technical scope; GET Catalog constitutes a community infrastructure resource.
added_value_to_my_research: Masked pretraining + LoRA fine-tuning paradigm -> blueprint for parameter-efficient adaptation of geospatial foundation models to new cities/regions with sparse labels
Jacobian-based region importance maps -> validated gradient attribution analogous to SHAP spatial heatmaps, motivating quasi-experimental validation in geospatial explainable ML
LiNGAM causal discovery on co-variation matrices -> urban regulatory grammar: directed causal graphs from spatiotemporal mobile-phone/POI data for physics-informed GNN
Leave-out generalization with one-shot fine-tuning (r>0.9) -> cross-city health model: pretrain on multi-city corpus, fine-tune on sparse target city for accessibility/telehealth prediction
Multi-modality fusion (ATAC + sequence + optional expression) -> blueprint for fusing mobile-phone, remote sensing, and POI data with modality-specific pretraining then joint fine-tuning

related.title: Multiomics and deep learning dissect regulatory syntax in human development
related.relation: companion paper on deep-learning decoding of regulatory grammar from multi-omic data in development

related.title: Regulatory grammar in human promoters uncovered by MPRA-based deep learning
related.relation: parallel lentiMPRA + deep learning approach to promoter regulatory grammar, validating GET's in silico MPRA strategy

related.title: A software framework for end-to-end genomic sequence analysis with deep learning
related.relation: end-to-end deep learning framework for genomic sequences providing methodological context for GET's architecture

related.title: Predicting drug responses of unseen cell types through transfer learning with foundation models
related.relation: transfer learning with foundation models for unseen cell-type generalization — directly parallel to GET's leave-out cell-type evaluation

related.title: Multi-task learning for medical foundation models
related.relation: multi-task pretraining strategy for biological foundation models — relevant to GET's proposed multi-modal extension

figures.caption: GET model architecture and applications — pretraining over 213 cell types and benchmark vs. baselines on leave-out astrocytes (Pearson r = 0.94)
Zero-shot in silico lentiMPRA — GET fine-tuned on K562 outperforms Enformer on 226,243-sequence regulatory activity benchmark
Long-range cis-regulatory element identification in fetal erythroblasts — GET finds distal (>1 Mbp) enhancers for fetal haemoglobin loci missed by prior models
Causal discovery of TF-TF interactions — GET Causal achieves 25.2% precision at top 1% of 79,242 possible pairs, outperforming motif colocalization baselines
PAX5-NR2C2 interaction in B cells — the leukaemia germline variant G183S in PAX5 IDR increases interaction with NR2C2, validated by proximity labelling assay
